title.en: Sometime, Sometimes, Some Time, Some Times

summary.en: Spacing and a final -s split four look-alikes: 'some time' is a stretch of time, 'sometime' means at some point, 'sometimes' means occasionally, and 'some times' means a few occasions.
summary.zh: 空格和词尾的 -s 把四个长得很像的词分开：some time 是一段时间，sometime 是某个时间点，sometimes 是有时候，some times 是几次。

heading.en: some time (two words) = a stretch of time
heading.zh: some time（分开写）= 一段时间

body.en: Written as two words, 'some' means 'a quantity of' and 'time' is the noun 'time', so 'some time' is simply a noun phrase meaning a period of time.
body.zh: 分开写时，some 是“一些”，time 是名词“时间”，所以 some time 就是一个名词短语，单纯指一段时间。

concepts.sourceRef: some time 是个名词短语，单纯指一段时间

heading.zh: sometime（合成一个词）= 在某个时间点

body.en: Merge the two words and 'sometime' is no longer a stretch of time but an adverb meaning 'at some unspecified point in time' — you can even bound it with a phrase like 'this afternoon'.
body.zh: 把 some 和 time 合并成一个词，sometime 就不再是一段时间，而是个副词，表示“在某个未指明的时间点”——你甚至可以加范围，比如 this afternoon。

concepts.sourceRef: sometime 不再像 some time 那样是个名词短语了，而是个副词

heading.zh: sometimes = 在某几个时间点 = 有时候

body.en: Add an -s and 'sometimes' is still an adverb, but the meaning shifts from one point in time to several — it happens a few times, how many we don't know, which is why we translate it as 'occasionally / 有时候'.
body.zh: 加上 -s，sometimes 仍是副词，但含义从某一个时间点变成某几个时间点——会发生几次，具体几次不知道，所以翻译成“有时候”。

concepts.sourceRef: sometimes 就是在某几个时间点

heading.en: some times (two words) = a few occasions

body.en: Split it again and the plural 'times' must be the countable noun meaning 'occasions' (not the uncountable 'time'). So 'some times' is a noun phrase meaning a few occasions. It is grammatical but rare — easily confused with 'sometimes' — so 'a few times' or 'a couple of times' is preferred.
body.zh: 再次分开，复数的 times 只能是可数名词“次数”（不是不可数的 time）。所以 some times 是个名词短语，表示几次。语法没错但少见——太容易和 sometimes 混淆——所以更常用 a few times 或 a couple of times。

concepts.sourceRef: 这里的 time 是次的意思

examples.text: I have some time now, so I'll visit the wolf.

note.en: 'Some time' is a noun phrase — a stretch of time you now have.
note.zh: “some time”是名词短语——你现在拥有的一段时间。

examples.text: Some time ago, I visited the wolf.

note.en: 'Some time ago' = a period of time in the past — still the noun phrase.
note.zh: “Some time ago”=之前一段时间——仍是名词短语。

examples.text: I will visit the wolf sometime.

note.en: 'Sometime' is an adverb: at some unspecified point in time.
note.zh: “sometime”是副词：在某个未指明的时间点。

examples.text: I will visit the wolf sometime this afternoon.

note.en: You can bound the adverb with a range — at some point within this afternoon.
note.zh: 可以给这个副词加范围——在今天下午的某个时间点。

note.en: 'Sometimes' is an adverb meaning occasionally — at several points in time.
note.zh: “sometimes”是副词，意为有时候——在某几个时间点。

examples.text: I visited the wolf some times last year.

note.en: Grammatical but awkward: 'some times' = a few occasions. 'A few times' is preferred.
note.zh: 语法对但拗口：“some times”=几次。更常用“a few times”。

examples.text: There were some times last week when I had to visit the wolf.

note.en: A natural use of the noun phrase 'some times' = a few occasions.
note.zh: 名词短语“some times”的自然用法=几次。

prompt.en: Which form fills the blank: 'I'll call you ____ next week to set up the meeting.'?
prompt.zh: 哪个形式填入空格：“I'll call you ____ next week to set up the meeting.”？

explain.en: An adverb meaning 'at some point next week' is needed — that is one-word 'sometime'.
explain.zh: 这里需要一个副词，表示“下周某个时间点”——也就是一个词的“sometime”。

prompt.en: In 'I have ____ before the train leaves', which best fits?
prompt.zh: 在“I have ____ before the train leaves”中，哪个最合适？

explain.en: The verb 'have' needs an object naming a stretch of time — the noun phrase 'some time'.
explain.zh: 动词“have”需要一个表示一段时间的宾语——名词短语“some time”。

prompt.en: Fill in one word meaning 'occasionally': '____ I visit the wolf.'
prompt.zh: 填一个表示“有时候”的词：“____ I visit the wolf.”

explain.en: 'Sometimes' is the adverb for 'occasionally / at several points in time'.
explain.zh: “sometimes”是表示“有时候/某几个时间点”的副词。

quizzes.sentence: Sometimes ago, I visited the wolf.
quizzes.fix: Some time ago, I visited the wolf.

explain.en: 'Ago' needs a noun phrase naming a period of time, so use two-word 'some time', not the adverb 'sometimes'.
explain.zh: “ago”前面需要一个表示一段时间的名词短语，所以要用分开写的“some time”，而不是副词“sometimes”。

quizzes.sentence: I visited the wolf three time last week.
quizzes.fix: I visited the wolf three times last week.

explain.en: When 'time' means an occasion, it is countable; after 'three' it must be the plural 'times'.
explain.zh: 当 time 表示“次”时是可数名词；在“three”后面必须用复数“times”。

title.zh: 一段对话用尽四个相似词

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Do you have some time to talk now?

note.en: Noun phrase: a stretch of time.

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Not now, but I'll drop by sometime this afternoon.

note.en: Adverb: at some point this afternoon.
note.zh: 副词：今天下午某个时间点。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: You only visit me sometimes — never enough!

note.en: Adverb: occasionally, at several points in time.
note.zh: 副词：有时候，某几个时间点。

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: There were some times last week when I really tried to come.
